title: daemon/api: handle_wallet_pay payment lock does not cover actual Zcash tx send
description: daemon/src/api.rs:259-346: handle_wallet_pay acquires wallet_pay_lock and does a balance check, then sends a PaymentAction::Request to the relay (informing the peer of intent to pay), then persists the session. The lock prevents double-session creation for the same amount. However: the actual Zcash spending transaction is not done inside this handler — it happens later when the peer responds with PaymentAction::Address. That later step does NOT acquire wallet_pay_lock. Two concurrent payment sessions could both pass the balance check, get addresses from the peer, and both attempt to send the full amount later (double-spend risk if balance is exactly equal to the requested amount). This design should be documented clearly in api.rs: the lock only prevents duplicate session creation, not duplicate spends. The actual spend path should also be audited.
